key_insight: RL post-training gains in these LLMs are concentrated in just a few transformer layers—often a single middle layer can recover most of the benefit of full-parameter RL.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a far cheaper and more targeted way to fine-tune large models for reasoning, code, and agentic tasks by updating only the most influential layers.
method: Define and measure "layer contribution" as the fraction of full RL improvement recovered when training one layer alone.
Run layer-wise RL experiments across 7 models from Qwen3 and Qwen2.5 using GRPO, GiGPO, and Dr. GRPO.
Compare rankings of contributing layers across tasks and settings to test stability of the pattern.
evidence_signal: Across seven models, three RL algorithms, and multiple domains, the authors report a stable layer-wise pattern with strong concentration of gains in middle transformer layers and high cross-task correlation of layer rankings.
watch_item: Single-layer training may work well for the studied setups, but the result may not transfer unchanged to other model families, scales, or RL objectives without validation.
paper_title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
